name: Strict-Transport-Security
description: Forces browsers to connect to the site over HTTPS only for the duration specified by max-age. Once received, browsers will refuse plain HTTP connections and automatically upgrade requests to HTTPS.
risk_if_missing: Users may connect over unencrypted HTTP, exposing session tokens, credentials, and data to network interception and man-in-the-middle attacks.

name: X-Frame-Options
description: Prevents the page from being rendered inside a frame, iframe, embed, or object element. DENY blocks all framing; SAMEORIGIN allows framing only by the same origin. Superseded by Content-Security-Policy frame-ancestors but still widely supported.
risk_if_missing: The site can be embedded in an attacker's iframe, enabling clickjacking attacks that trick users into performing unintended actions.

name: X-Content-Type-Options
description: Prevents browsers from MIME-sniffing a response's content type away from the declared Content-Type. With nosniff, browsers will block requests if the MIME type does not match the expected type for the resource context.
risk_if_missing: Browsers may interpret files with incorrect MIME types as executable scripts, enabling content injection and cross-site scripting via uploaded files.

name: X-XSS-Protection
description: Legacy header that activated the built-in reflected XSS filter in older browsers (IE, older Chrome/Safari). Modern recommendation is to set it to 0 (disabled) and rely on Content-Security-Policy instead, as the filter itself introduced vulnerabilities.
risk_if_missing: Not setting it explicitly may leave it in an inconsistent state in old browsers. Setting 1; mode=block on old sites added a partial XSS defense but is obsolete.

name: Content-Security-Policy
description: Defines trusted sources for all resource types (scripts, styles, images, fonts, frames, etc.). Effectively mitigates cross-site scripting (XSS), data injection, and clickjacking by restricting what the browser is permitted to load and execute.
risk_if_missing: Without CSP, injected inline scripts execute freely, enabling XSS attacks that can steal cookies, credentials, and sensitive data or perform actions on behalf of the user.

name: Content-Security-Policy-Report-Only
description: Like Content-Security-Policy but does not enforce — it only reports violations to the specified report-uri or report-to endpoint. Used to test CSP policies before enforcing them in production.
risk_if_missing: Without this header, organizations cannot gather violation reports to iteratively build a CSP policy without risk of breaking the site.

name: Referrer-Policy
description: Controls how much referrer information is sent with requests. strict-origin-when-cross-origin sends full URL for same-origin, only the origin for cross-origin HTTPS, and nothing for HTTP destinations from HTTPS.
risk_if_missing: The Referer header may leak sensitive URL parameters (tokens, IDs, search terms) to third-party sites loaded on your pages.

name: Permissions-Policy
description: Formerly Feature-Policy. Controls which browser features and APIs the page and embedded iframes are allowed to use (camera, microphone, geolocation, payment, etc.). Disabling unused features reduces the attack surface.
risk_if_missing: Embedded third-party scripts and iframes can access powerful browser APIs without restriction, enabling potential privacy violations and capability abuse.

name: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy
description: Prevents a document from loading cross-origin resources that do not grant explicit permission via CORS or CORP headers. Required alongside COOP to enable SharedArrayBuffer and high-resolution timers.
risk_if_missing: Without COEP (in conjunction with COOP), access to SharedArrayBuffer and precise timers is blocked by browsers as a Spectre mitigation.

name: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy
description: Isolates the browsing context group so cross-origin windows cannot reference this page via window.opener. Prevents Spectre-based cross-origin information leaks and enables use of SharedArrayBuffer.
risk_if_missing: Cross-origin popups can access the window object, enabling Spectre-based side-channel attacks and unauthorized cross-origin script access.

name: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy
description: Prevents other origins from loading this response's resource via no-cors requests. Defends against Spectre attacks where cross-origin resources are read via timing side channels.
risk_if_missing: Resources (images, scripts, APIs) can be loaded by cross-origin pages in no-cors mode, potentially leaking sensitive data via timing attacks.

name: Cache-Control
description: Controls how responses are cached by browsers and intermediary proxies. For sensitive pages (authentication, account data), no-store prevents any caching. For static assets, max-age with immutable is recommended.
risk_if_missing: Sensitive pages or API responses may be cached by browsers or shared proxies, allowing subsequent users on shared devices to view another user's data.

name: Clear-Site-Data
description: Instructs the browser to clear cookies, localStorage, sessionStorage, and caches for the origin when this response is received. Typically sent on logout endpoints to ensure complete session termination.
risk_if_missing: After logout, residual cookies, cached data, or stored tokens may persist and be reused to re-authenticate without credentials.

name: NEL
description: Network Error Logging — instructs browsers to report network-level errors (DNS failures, TCP resets, TLS errors) to a configured reporting endpoint. Helps diagnose connectivity and TLS issues affecting real users.
risk_if_missing: Network-level failures affecting users are invisible to server-side monitoring, making it difficult to detect and diagnose connectivity issues.

name: Report-To
description: Configures named reporting endpoints for use with CSP report-to, NEL, COEP, and COOP violation reports. Part of the Reporting API specification.
risk_if_missing: Browser security policy violations and network errors cannot be reported to a centralized collection endpoint for monitoring and alerting.

name: Access-Control-Allow-Origin
description: Specifies which origins are permitted to read the response in cross-origin browser requests. Using * allows any origin; restrict to specific trusted origins for authenticated APIs.
risk_if_missing: Browsers block cross-origin fetch/XHR responses by default (CORS). Without this header, legitimate cross-origin clients cannot access the API.

name: Access-Control-Allow-Methods
description: In a CORS preflight response, lists the HTTP methods allowed for cross-origin requests. Sent in response to an OPTIONS preflight request.
risk_if_missing: Cross-origin clients issuing non-simple methods (PUT, DELETE, PATCH) will be blocked by browsers after a preflight failure, breaking API functionality.

name: Access-Control-Allow-Headers
description: In a CORS preflight response, specifies which request headers the browser is permitted to include in cross-origin requests.
risk_if_missing: Cross-origin requests with custom headers (Authorization, Content-Type for JSON, etc.) will be rejected by browsers during preflight checks.

name: Access-Control-Max-Age
description: Specifies how long (in seconds) the browser should cache the results of a CORS preflight request, reducing the number of OPTIONS requests sent for subsequent cross-origin calls.
risk_if_missing: Without caching, browsers send a preflight OPTIONS request before every cross-origin non-simple request, adding latency and server load.

name: Expect-CT
description: Deprecated as of 2021. Previously instructed browsers to enforce and/or report Certificate Transparency requirements for the site's TLS certificate. Modern browsers enforce CT automatically, making this header unnecessary.
risk_if_missing: No current risk — CT enforcement is now handled natively by browsers without this header. Keeping it is harmless but redundant.
